stance: BULLISH (AIP-driven hyper-growth) — high MCS, premium valuation
summary: Palantir is the highest-growth and highest-MCS name in the coverage universe: 8 consecutive next-quarter revenue guidance beats (MCS 1.000 simple / 0.83 info-adjusted) with revenue accelerating from +21% YoY (Q1 FY24) to +84.7% YoY (Q1 FY26). FY26 guidance was raised in Q1 to $7.65-7.66B representing +71% growth and a Rule of 40 score of 129%. Operating margin expanded from 12.7% (Q1 FY24) to 46.2% (Q1 FY26). The risk is valuation: P/E FY1 95× sits +152% above peer median and EV/EBITDA FY1 75× is +154% above peer median — the multiple requires sustained 50%+ growth to defend. Stock has corrected -33.6% from $207.52 ATH (Nov 3 2025) to $137.80 (May 8 2026); MACD bearish, sma50 < sma200 since 2026-02-27. Sell-side target $189.46 = +38.4% upside; 31 brokers, Overweight (1.47).
quotes: On the back of this continued strength in the U.S., we are raising our full year 2026 revenue guidance midpoint to $7.656 billion, representing 71% growth year-over-year, a 10-point increase over our full year 2026 revenue guidance from last quarter and our largest ever full year revenue guidance raise.
We are raising our adjusted free cash flow guidance to between $4.2 billion and $4.4 billion, and we continue to expect GAAP operating income and net income in each quarter of this year.

We also generated our highest ever reported revenue growth rate of 63% year-over-year exceeding the high end of our prior guidance, a 1,300 basis points and representing a 3,300 basis point increase compared to the growth rate in Q3 of last year.

We are also raising our full year 2025 revenue guidance midpoint to $4.146 billion, representing a 45% year-over-year growth rate, a 9-point increase over our full year 2025 revenue guidance last quarter, and our largest ever full year revenue guidance raise.

The ceiling for our Maven Smart System contract was increased by $795 million to prepare for what they expect will be significant demand from combatant commands for our AI-powered software capabilities over the next 4 years.
With our 2026 revenue and adjusted operating income guidance, we are guiding to a Rule of 40 score of 129% for the full year.
bull: Eight consecutive next-quarter revenue guidance beats, all above the high end of the range. Average beat magnitude +5.85% over the high end. MCS 1.000 simple, 0.83 information-adjusted — the highest in coverage.
Revenue growth went from +21% YoY (Q1 FY24) to +84.7% YoY (Q1 FY26). Q4 FY25 actual +75% YoY. US Commercial growth guidance for FY26 raised to +120%.
Operating margin expanded 33.5pp from 12.7% (Q1 FY24) to 46.2% (Q1 FY26). FCF margin reached 54.6% in Q1 FY26 — among the highest in software.
AIP boot-camps converting at high rates; US Commercial revenue trajectory drove the FY26 guide raise. Foundry + AIP at $1B+ ARR runrate.
Maven ceiling raised $795M in Q2 FY25 for combatant command demand. PLTR remains the dominant software platform for DoD AI deployments.
$8.45B stockholders equity; $7.8B+ net cash (Net Debt -$7,824M per FactSet). Current ratio 6.91× — flush with capital and no refinancing risk.
bear: P/E FY1 95× = +152% premium vs Software peer median 37.87×. EV/EBITDA FY1 75× = +154% premium vs 29.56×. Multiple compression risk if growth decelerates below 50%.
FactSet target $189.46 = +38.4% upside, but well below the $207.52 ATH (Nov 2025). Implied analyst caution despite ratings averaging Overweight (1.47).
Death cross (SMA50 below SMA200) on 2026-02-27 still active. Stock -33.6% from ATH. RSI 41.7 — pressured. ADX 11.25 = no strong trend (sideways/down).
US Govt >40% of revenue. Maven, ICE, DoD contracts subject to administration changes, appropriation cycles. Top commercial customers >20% concentration.
SBC running ~$300M+/quarter (~20% of revenue). Share count growth offsets buybacks; dual-class structure limits minority influence.
Karp's public political profile creates contract risk in EU/Asia. Continued insider sales (10b5-1 plans) by Karp/Thiel/Cohen. Limited Class A voting rights.
trigger_up: Q2 FY26 ER (Aug 10, 2026) beats guide AND raises FY26 above $7.66B midpoint
Operating margin sustains above 45% — confirming structural leverage
US Commercial revenue acceleration (>100% YoY for 2 consecutive quarters)
New Maven / Tradewinds / IL6 ceiling expansions disclosed
Recovery above SMA200 ($163.93) confirms trend resumption to $200+
trigger_down: Q2 FY26 guide raise <$1.8B at low end (current guide $1.797-1.801B) — signaling deceleration
Rule of 40 score falls below 110% on FY27 outlook (vs current 129%)
US Govt budget freeze or Maven contract scaling delays
Break below 52w low $112.07 — major technical breakdown to $90 area
PE FY1 reverts toward 60× (~38% downside) on growth deceleration concerns
bottom_line: Palantir is the highest-conviction operational story in coverage — 8/8 MCS, +85% accelerating revenue growth, 46% operating margins, Rule-of-40 of 129% — but it is also the most expensive (P/E FY1 95×, +152% premium vs Software peers). The 33.6% drawdown from November 2025 highs has reduced near-term technical risk but the bearish MACD/SMA cross is still active. Sell-side target $189.46 = +38.4% upside reflects analyst caution on valuation. Key catalyst: Q2 FY26 ER on Aug 10, 2026, where a $1.8B+ guide and FY26 raise above $7.66B midpoint would defend the multiple. A guide miss or deceleration to <50% YoY growth would compress the multiple meaningfully. Tactical bias: BULLISH on operational quality, MIXED on entry timing given valuation; scale-in below $130 looks favorable risk/reward.
disclaimer: Quoted statements verbatim from PLTR earnings calls in /PLTR/Transcripts/ directory. Fundamentals from EDGAR XBRL companyfacts (CIK 0001321655). FactSet peer data captured 2026-05-12. Not investment advice.
